text.title: Supabase Backup and Restore Drill Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Supabase Backup and Restore Drill methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Restore drill MUST run at least every 90 days. Drill freshness MUST be ≤90 days before any deploy that depends on backup viability (e.g. r2-backup-before-migration in solo-deploy-checklist).
rule.rationale: Backups age into unrestorable states quietly; only a drill catches it.

rule.statement: Restore target MUST be a clean / empty Supabase project — NEVER the production project. Wrong-target restore overwrites live data.
rule.rationale: Restore is destructive; a misclick overwrites production. Separate target eliminates the failure mode.

rule.statement: Post-restore validation MUST include row-count per table AND md5sum (or pg_dump diff) against the source. row_count_delta = 0 is required to pass.
rule.rationale: A restore that 'completes' without errors can still drop rows silently (FK violations skipped). Checksum is proof.

rule.statement: DrillReport names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
DrillReport records: drill_date, restore_seconds, source_snapshot_id, target_project, row_count_delta, owner. Missing any field invalidates the drill.
rule.rationale: Untraced drills can't be audited; trust without evidence is the gap we're closing.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-supabase-backup-and-restore-drill.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
